def: An accountant; under Constantine, an officer of the empire; areceiver of revenue; an administrator of a department.
Feudal tenure; the feudal system. See Feudality. Burke.
One who lends.The borrower is servant to the lender. Prov. xxii. 7.
An explosive consisting of gun cotton and camphor dissolved innitroglycerin. [Written also nitrogelatine.]
To be moved, or to pass, on a buoyant medium; to float.And now the shouts waft near the citadel. Dryden.
To throw as a lance; to hurl; to dart. [R.]While thy unerring hand elanced . . . a dart. Prior.

An oil used for illuminating purposes, formerly obtained fromthe distillation of mineral wax, bituminous shale, etc., and hencecalled also coal oil. It is now produced in immense quantities,chiefly by the distillation and purification of petroleum. Itconsists chiefly of several hydrocarbons of the methane series.
The quality of being deft. Drayton.
With a sense of loss. [Obs.] Shak.
Having six rays; -- said of certain sponge spicules. SeeIllust. of Spicule.
